Title: Add secondary “Category / Field Name” filter below Advanced Filters in DAM search results
Description:
After a user performs a search using the main DAM search bar, the system displays the “Advanced Filters” section along with the search results.

However, users currently cannot filter based on which metadata field contains the searched term.

A new Category / Field Name filter block should be added below Advanced Filters.
This filter must:

  • Display metadata fields in the same chip/tag style as other filters

  • Allow selection of one or multiple fields

  • Update the results so that the keyword must be found in the selected metadata field(s)

This improves accuracy and makes it easier for users to find assets based on where the keyword exists.

Example

User searches for: “Vietnamese”
User selects Category / Field Name: Language

→ The results update to show only assets where the metadata field “Language” contains the keyword "Vietnamese."
Acceptance Criteria:
1 — Filter Visibility

  • The “Category / Field Name” filter block appears below the "Advanced Filters" section after any keyword search is performed.

  • The filter block is visible by default (not hidden, not collapsed).

2 — Field Selection

  • The filter block displays a list of all searchable metadata fields, such as:

    • Title

    • Description

    • Tags

    • Language

    • Custom metadata fields

  • For each field, the system shows a count of how many assets contain the searched keyword in that specific field.

  • Users can select one or multiple metadata fields and while selecting multiple filters by default AND logic applies

Example

User searches for "Apple"

The system finds assets where "Apple" appears in different metadata fields.

So the filter block shows something like:

  • Title (3) → “Apple” appears in the title of 3 assets

  • Description (6) → “Apple” appears in the description of 6 assets

  • Tags (10) → “Apple” appears in the tags of 10 assets

  • Language (0) → No assets have “Apple” in the language field

  • Custom Field: Category (4) → “Apple” appears in the Category field of 4 assets

These fields appear as selectable options.

User selects:

✔ Title (3)

What happens:
The system now shows only the 3 assets where “Apple” appears in the Title field.

User also selects:

✔ Tags (10)
(AND logic applies by default)

What happens:
The system now shows assets where “Apple” appears in:

  • Title
    AND

  • Tags

Suppose only 2 assets match both conditions → the results update to show 2 items.


3 — Filter Behavior

  • When a metadata field is selected, search results automatically update to show only assets where the searched keyword appears in that specific metadata field.

  • Multiple selected fields respect the existing filter logic OR, and the selected Metadata attributes are highlighted, and unselected options also remain visible to the user, but not highlighted.

4 — UI / UX

  • The filter block visually matches the design of the Advanced Filters area.

  • Each selected metadata field displays as a removable chip/tag inside the filter bar.

  • Removing a chip immediately updates the results.

5 — Saving Searches

  • Clicking “Save Search” saves all selected Category / Field Name filters along with other search parameters.

  • Re-opening a saved search restores:

    • Keyword

    • Advanced Filters

    • Category / Field Name field selections

6 — Clear Behavior

  • Clicking “Clear Search” removes:

    • The keyword

    • All Advanced Filters

    • All Category / Field Name selections
